edges.description: Reduced ribosome biogenesis activates nucleolar stress, stabilizing p53 and triggering apoptosis in neural crest cells.
edges.source: Impaired Ribosome Biogenesis in Neural Crest Cells

edges.description: Depletion of cranial neural crest cells leads to deficient formation of first and second pharyngeal arch-derived craniofacial structures.

nodes.description: Neonatal airway management including special positioning, nasopharyngeal airways, or tracheostomy for infants with significant upper airway obstruction.

evidence.snippet: Neonates with airway issues may require airway management at delivery, special positioning, or tracheostomy to facilitate ventilation.

nodes.description: Conductive hearing loss affecting approximately 40-50% of individuals, due to malformation of the ossicles and hypoplasia of the middle ear cavities.

evidence.snippet: About 40%-50% of individuals have conductive hearing loss attributed most commonly to malformation of the ossicles and hypoplasia of the middle ear cavities.

nodes.description: Depletion of cranial neural crest cells results in bilateral symmetric hypoplasia of the zygomatic bones, maxilla, and mandible, as well as external and middle ear malformations and orbital abnormalities.

evidence.snippet: Treacher Collins syndrome (TCS) is characterized by lower eyelid abnormalities, malar hypoplasia, downslanted palpebral fissures, and micro- or retrognathia due to symmetric hypoplasia of the zygomatic bones, maxilla, and mandible.

nodes.description: Mutations in TCOF1/POLR1B/POLR1C/POLR1D impair ribosomal RNA transcription and ribosome biogenesis. Neural crest cells are uniquely sensitive to reduced ribosome output due to their high proliferative and migratory demands during embryonic craniofacial development.
nodes.id: Impaired Ribosome Biogenesis in Neural Crest Cells

evidence.reference_title: Treacher Collins Syndrome: the genetics of a craniofacial disease.
evidence.snippet: The association of the TCOF1 gene product, Treacle, and gene products of POLR1C and POLR1D with ribosome biosynthesis suggests that a loss of function mutation in these genes disrupts ribosome biosynthesis in constituent neural crest cells and neuroepithelium leading to apoptosis.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of TCOF1 Gene in Health and Disease: Beyond Treacher Collins Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: One of the crucial proteins involved in most of the key nucleolar functions is treacle phosphoprotein encoded by the TCOF1 gene.

nodes.description: Speech therapy as part of multimodal hearing-loss support for individuals with conductive hearing impairment.

evidence.snippet: Hearing loss is treated with bone conduction amplification, speech therapy, and educational intervention.

nodes.description: Respiratory difficulties from mandibular hypoplasia and glossoptosis, which may require airway management at birth.

evidence.snippet: Neonates with airway issues may require airway management at delivery, special positioning, or tracheostomy to facilitate ventilation.

nodes.description: Nucleolar stress from impaired ribosome biogenesis stabilizes p53 in neural crest cells, triggering apoptosis during the critical window of craniofacial development. p53 heterozygosity is protective, confirming p53's central role in TCS pathogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Treacher Collins Syndrome: the genetics of a craniofacial disease.
evidence.snippet: P53 heterozygosity is protective against TCS, and that P53 and TCOF1 hemizygous embryos do not affect ribosomal function, implicates P53 or elements downstream of P53 as playing a role in TCS pathogenesis.
